sourceMovie.title: 21 Bridges
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto 21 Bridges (2019) as a crime theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The crime and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Havoc
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If 21 Bridges clicked for you, Havoc (2025) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and action, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: JCVD
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If 21 Bridges clicked for you, JCVD (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and action, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after 21 Bridges?
faq.answer: Try Havoc, JCVD, Coogan's Bluff—each shares DNA with 21 Bridges while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is 21 Bridges?
faq.answer: 21 Bridges (2019) is best described as Crime, Action, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is 21 Bridges worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
